label: National Rail – Normal Service – Single or Return

cancellation_windows.label: Early cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested 48 or more hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: Standard cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested between 24 and 48 hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: Late cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested between 2 and 24 hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested less than 2 hours before departure, or after departure time has passed

return_ticket_notes: For return tickets, each leg is assessed independently. If the outbound leg has already been travelled, only the return leg amount is eligible for refund, assessed against the return departure time.
no_show_policy: Passengers who do not travel and do not cancel in advance are treated as a no-show. No refund is issued for no-shows.

label: National Rail – Express Service – Single or Return

cancellation_windows.label: Early cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested 48 or more hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: Late cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested between 24 and 48 hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested less than 24 hours before departure, or after departure time has passed

return_ticket_notes: Same as normal service: each leg assessed independently against its own departure time.
no_show_policy: No refund issued for no-shows on express services.

label: Metro – Single Ticket

cancellation_windows.label: Full refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested before the passenger has tapped in at the origin station
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'confirmed' and journey has not commenced

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Journey has already commenced (passenger has tapped in)
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'completed' or travelled_at is not null

notes: Metro single tickets are valid on the travel date only. A booking with status 'cancelled' is already cancelled and no further refund action is needed.

label: Metro – Day Pass

cancellation_windows.label: Full refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested before the passenger has made their first tap-in on the travel date
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'confirmed' and travelled_at is null

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Day pass has been activated (first tap-in recorded)
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'completed' or travelled_at is not null

notes: Day passes are non-transferable and valid only for the calendar day of purchase.

label: Delay Compensation – All Networks

compensation_rules.condition: Train delayed 30–59 minutes due to operator fault
compensation_rules.compensation: 50% refund of the fare for the delayed leg
compensation_rules.how_to_claim: Submit claim via app or customer service within 28 days of travel date

compensation_rules.condition: Train delayed 60–119 minutes due to operator fault
compensation_rules.compensation: 100% refund of the fare for the delayed leg
compensation_rules.how_to_claim: Submit claim via app or customer service within 28 days of travel date

compensation_rules.condition: Train delayed 120 minutes or more due to operator fault
compensation_rules.compensation: 100% refund of the fare for the delayed leg, plus reimbursement of alternative transport costs up to $10.00
compensation_rules.how_to_claim: Submit claim with receipts via customer service within 28 days of travel date

exclusions: Delays caused by third-party events (extreme weather, security incidents, government directives, or passenger-caused incidents) are not eligible for compensation.

label: Delay Compensation - Easy Reference
content: DELAY COMPENSATION SUMMARY: For delays of 30 to 59 minutes, you get 50% refund. For delays of 60 to 119 minutes, you get 100% refund. For delays of 120 minutes or more, you get 100% refund plus up to $10 for alternative transport. Claim within 28 days. Delays must be operator fault. Excludes weather, security, and passenger-caused delays.
summary: 30-59 min = 50% refund. 60-119 min = 100% refund. 120+ min = 100% refund + $10.

label: Planned Service Disruption – Engineering Works
overview: When a service is cancelled or significantly altered due to planned engineering works, passengers are entitled to a full refund or free rebooking. Planned disruptions are announced at least 7 days in advance via the app, website, and station notices.

refund_rules.condition: Service fully cancelled by operator due to planned engineering works
refund_rules.entitlement: 100% full refund of the ticket price. No admin fee applied.
refund_rules.alternative: Free rebooking on the next available service on the same route at no extra cost.
refund_rules.how_to_claim: Request via the app under 'My Journeys → Disruption Refund', website, or customer service desk within 28 days of the original travel date.

refund_rules.condition: Service operates via a replacement route and journey time increases by 30 minutes or more
refund_rules.entitlement: 50% refund of the affected leg's fare.
refund_rules.how_to_claim: Submit claim via app under 'Disruptions' or contact customer service within 28 days.

refund_rules.condition: Passenger chooses not to travel because of announced engineering works, but the service is still running
refund_rules.entitlement: Refund subject to the standard cancellation policy (RF001 for normal service, RF002 for express). Engineering works announcements do not override the standard cancellation windows.
refund_rules.note: If the service is still running, the standard policy applies regardless of the disruption announcement.

advance_notice: Planned engineering works are announced at least 7 days in advance. Passengers with affected bookings receive an in-app notification and email automatically.
no_refund_cases: No additional compensation is provided for inconvenience beyond the entitlements listed above.

label: Missed Connection – Operator-Caused Delay
overview: If a passenger misses a connecting TransitFlow service because their incoming TransitFlow service was delayed, they are entitled to free rebooking and may be eligible for delay compensation.

rules.condition: Passenger misses a connecting TransitFlow service because their incoming TransitFlow service was delayed by the operator
rules.entitlement: Free rebooking on the next available service to the original destination. No new ticket required.
rules.how_to_claim: Show original ticket and delay evidence to platform staff, or contact customer service within 24 hours of travel.

rules.condition: Missed connection results in a total end-to-end journey delay of 30 minutes or more at the final destination
rules.entitlement: Compensation under RF005 (Delay Compensation – All Networks) applies to the total delay, measured from the originally scheduled arrival time to actual arrival time at the final destination.
rules.note: Delay is measured end-to-end, not per leg.

exclusions: This policy covers only connections between TransitFlow services. Missed connections to third-party transport (buses, taxis, other rail operators) are not covered.
